insights.text: The core issue with retirement plans is their dependence on new workers to support current retirees, creating a demographic problem in the long term.
insights.quote: the only way it's running is because people arepaying it in the problem is there's not enough money coming inbecause rememberwe talked about the beginning the demographicsthere's less and less young peoplethere's less and less young people
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Investing Vs Real Estate Vs Crypto Debate: The Retirement Crisis Is Coming & They’re Lying To You About Renting!
insights.guest: Roaul Pal, Humphrey Yang, Jaspreet Singh

insights.text: Rewiring your brain requires awareness of existing patterns and triggers, often tracing back to understand the root cause of unwanted behaviors or moods.
insights.quote: In order to do that you have to recognize when your brain is doing a pattern that you want to rewire and quite often like it's already triggered so many things and like you're in a bad mood and you have no idea why
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Maisie Williams: The Painful Past Of A Game Of Thrones Star

insights.text: Bitcoin is establishing itself as a new asset class and a global monetary system, with potential for substantial growth, particularly in emerging markets.
insights.quote: It is a new asset class. It does represent a global monetary system unlike any other digital asset out there.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - World's No.1 Investing Expert: The Big Shift Is Coming And  This Investment Could Be 15x in 5 Years! - Cathie Wood

insights.text: To prevent the torture of wanting a result without the commitment, determine if you desire the lifestyle that leads to that outcome.
insights.quote: To want the outcome without the lifestyle is to like torture yourself...the question is, do you want the lifestyle?
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Discipline Expert: The Habit That Will Make Or Break Your Entire 2026! James Clear

insights.text: Consistency and persistence are often more important than pure talent or skill.
insights.quote: But outlasting people and consistency is massively underrated.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Tom Aspinall: The UFC Doesn't Want You To Know This! Jon Jones Wasn't Living Like An Athlete!

insights.text: It is not vital to have competence in the industry of the business you're trying to buy; knowing how to do deals and finding experts to consult with is more important.
insights.quote: Not really, no. Not at all...More important than anything is it's sort of like real estate. It's really about the deal.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Money Expert: From $0 to Millions In 2 Years Without Any Hard Work!: Codie Sanchez

insights.text: Instead of searching for meaning, one can create meaning by choosing a path of continuous learning and reducing the suffering of others.
insights.quote: I recognized long ago that in a free society...I have the power to manufacture meaning in my life...For me, a meaningful life is learning something new tomorrow that I didn't know yesterday...spend a little bit of your life lessening the suffering of others.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Neil deGrasse Tyson: DO THIS Every Morning To Find Happiness & Meaning In Your Life

insights.text: It's vital to create a company where employees feel proud to work, which boosts morale and performance.
insights.quote: It's really important that, you know, if somebody's in a pub and they work for Virgin and somebody says, what do you do, that they're proud of the fact that, you know, they work for Virgin and they're happy to say it.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Richard Branson: How A Dyslexic Drop-out Built A Billion Dollar Empire

insights.text: Confidence is built incrementally through small wins that validate your abilities, creating a staircase towards larger achievements.
insights.quote: It's the confidence in the small stuff that actually builds the confidence towards the big stuff.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Classpass Founder: Quitting My 9-5 Led To A $1 Billion Business: Payal Kadakia

insights.text: True fulfillment arises from mastery and being present, not from deferred achievements or external validation like money.
insights.quote: Fundamentally happiness and fulfillment comesfrom mastery as i said before being in the present moment. Now, the money that Leon requires is the byproduct of the mastery, and it is not the objective.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E14: John Vincent - Building A Restaurant Empire

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley
Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: Your reaction to circumstances, not the circumstances themselves, defines your outcome.
insights.quote: Your reaction is your result, not the circumstance.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E11: I have a huge announcement to make...

insights.text: The ability to endure rejection is more crucial for success than talent or even hard work.
insights.quote: The ability to endure that rejection is absolutely the key to success. More so than talent, more so than I even, I wouldn't say hard work, I'd say grit is right up there.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Relationships Professor: “This Is An Emergency!” The Number Of Men Having No Sex Increased 180%! (Why This Is Dangerous & How We Fix It)

insights.text: Evaluate your position in the workplace, if you are being undervalued, make your case, and if you are not heard then look for another job.
insights.quote: Here's what I could do for you if you gave me a 40% raise and the opportunity to progress,but I'd like to see a plan for that. And I've been looking for other opportunities before conducting this discussion, and I have some.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Jordan Peterson: How To Become The Person You’ve Always Wanted To Be

insights.text: Eating a variety of plants, even nuts and seeds, is crucial for a diverse and healthy gut microbiome.
insights.quote: People forget that a plant doesn't have to be doesn't look like spinach or kale. It can be a nut and a seed, which are so nutrient dense and so useful...
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Food Doctor: "Extra Protein Is Making You Fatter!" The 6 Food Lies Everyone Still Believes! (Brand NEW Food Science) - Tim Spector

insights.text: The greatest risk of AI isn't its potential for harm, but failing to rapidly implement it to address global issues.
insights.quote: My actual fear is we're not goingto adopt it fast enough to solve the problems thataffect everybody.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Google CEO: AI Is Creating Deadly Viruses! If We See This, We Must Turn Off AI! They Leaked Our Secrets At Google!

insights.text: Leaders must provide a clear and consistent vision, enabling individuals to work towards a shared goal with less direct supervision.
insights.quote: In the end, what you want is that the job of a leader is to make the target, the aim, the final destination, whatever, that clear, like the sun, that everybody's automatically going there and you don't have to push them every day.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Jürgen Klopp: Would You Go Back To Manage LFC...? The Real Reason I Fell In Love With Liverpool!

insights.text: Eating food in a specific order (veggies, protein/fats, then starches/sugars) can reduce glucose spikes by up to 75% without changing the meal.
insights.quote: If you eat the ingredients in the meal in a specific order, you can reduce the glucose spike of that meal by up to 75% without changing how much you're eating, what you're eating.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Scary New Research On Sugar & How They Made You Addicted To It! Jessie Inchauspé

insights.text: Mutual respect, honesty, and trust are fundamental, but it's also vital to recognize and accept that you are not the same person and have different interests.
insights.quote: There has to be a level of mutual respect there and honesty and trust and all of those things. That goes without saying, but you're not the same person.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 66 - Karren Brady's Secret To A Long Lasting Relationship

insights.text: Most sickness prevalent in society is not genetic but related to lifestyle and diet changes over the last century.
insights.quote: Very little of the sickness that we see so prevalent in society around us is truly genetic.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - No.1 Heart Surgeon: Cardio Is A Waste Of Time For Weight Loss! Philip Ovadia

insights.text: Young men are facing challenges like loneliness and sexual dysfunction due to social atrophy and reliance on pornography, highlighting a need for real-world connection.
insights.quote: The majority of men who come to sex therapy today for erectile dysfunctions are young men in their 20s, not old men after prostate issues...Because they spend an enormous amount of time with themselves, watching porn and masturbating.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Sex Expert (Esther Perel): The Relationship Crisis No One Talks About That's Killing Your Sex Life!

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley

insights.text: Charisma and confidence are learnable skills, not inherent traits, that dramatically impact both external success and internal well-being.
insights.quote: So it is most definitely something you can learn. I feel like I'm a testament to that.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Charisma Teacher: Psychology Of Why People Don't Like You! People Are Attracted To These Traits! The Secret To Being Instantly Likeable!

insights.text: There is a potential for humans to communicate with those that have passed away.
insights.quote: I couldn't speak about it until now, that it's possible to communicate with someone that's passed away.And I'm saying it from the point of view of being a neuroscientist and a psychiatrist.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Neuroscience Expert Dr. Tara Swart on Evidence We Can Communicate After Death and Her Experience Speaking to the Dead!

insights.text: True optimism in entrepreneurship means refusing to accept 'no' and always seeking a way to achieve goals.
insights.quote: I just think there's always a way there's always a way and your life and my life is testament to this.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - NotOnTheHighStreet.com Founder: Rapid Success Lead To My Darkest Days: Holly Tucker

insights.text: Functional depression can exist even when someone achieves external success, but unresolved emotions will still be carried.
insights.quote: I was like a functional depressive this whole time... I wasn't happy. It was heavy. Taking on everyone's emotions... and then I'd be home in the evening and be like, whoa, I don't want to talk to anyone.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Alex Scott: I’ve Never Told The FULL Truth About My Past

insights.text: Authenticity is the most valuable asset for brands, and abandoning it in pursuit of trends can alienate audiences.
insights.quote: Your authenticity is your cultural currency the minute your audience can no longer see your original pursuit... is the day they kind of leave you and go go engage and partner with someone else that's in part because you've left yourself right in the pursuit of cool you've abandoned your authenticity
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Marketing Genius Behind Nike: Greg Hoffman

insights.text: True vulnerability requires difficult, face-to-face conversations rather than broadcasting emotions on social media.
insights.quote: Do that exact same thing with the person you hurt. That is way more difficult. Don't leave a voice memo saying, hey, I'm really sorry, just taking accountability. Call them or go visit them and look them in the eye and say that exact same sentence that you just left a voice memo for. That's vulnerability.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Simon Sinek: The Number One Reason Why You’re Not Succeeding

insights.text: Learning is more effectively done in direction. A young person wanting to work in a field, should find experts in that field to learn from.
insights.quote: Go find someone that you admire.Go find a master who's doing the work that you want to emulate and put five years in working for them.Don't go try to start your own project right away.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Your Bones Break First: The Man Who Survived Being Eaten Alive!

insights.text: Your intuition is a muscle that needs to be trained; quiet external voices and societal expectations to allow your intuition to guide you.
insights.quote: It is like any muscle. You know, you got to work it. You've got to listen to it. You have to make it brave to talk to you.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - CMO Of Netflix, Uber & Apple! Work Life Balance Is BAD Advice! "I Lost My Baby & My Husband!" - Bozoma Saint John

insights.text: Success comes from shutting out the internal noise and self-doubt rather than external criticism.
insights.quote: What actually brings you to success and self-worth and happiness is actually understanding how to stand at that plate and shut out the noise that's here. Not the millions of people around, the millions of people are in your head screaming at you.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Scooter Braun: My Divorce Saved My Life, But I Didn’t See It Coming! I Still Carry Guilt For The Young Artists I Managed!

insights.text: The brain's primary function is to keep you safe, and its secondary function is to help you achieve things, but it is also designed to make you happy, as happiness is the optimal state for survival and performance.
insights.quote: The primary function of the brain is to make you safe okay and then the secondary function that we push human as humans to that brain to do is to invent iphones and create podcasts and have amazing things right that's a secondary function but believe it or not before that secondary function your your brain is supposed to make you happy because happy is the ultimate form for you to perform in life.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - 4 Moments On The Diary Of A CEO That Changed My Life

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley
Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: Constructive criticism and feedback affirm belonging and demonstrate care.
insights.quote: When I say to them, this isn't good work and here's how it can be better, I am affirming to them that they are part of my team.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Malcolm Gladwell: Working From Home Is Destroying Us!

insights.text: There is an absence of enforcement of international law, leading to a 'victor's justice' where dominant powers act as they wish.
insights.quote: That's the point to be made here is that there's an absence of the enforcement of law internationally.And it's victor's justice.And the dominant will essentially exercise whatever will they want.the law be damned.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - WW3 Threat Assessment: "Trump Bombing Iran Just Increased Nuclear War Threat" The Terrifying Reality
insights.guest: Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: Early childhood experiences, particularly relational trauma stemming from a lack of acknowledgment and honor, profoundly shapes one's identity and perception of the world.
insights.quote: You become how people see you.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Body Trauma Expert: Medicating Kids Can Harm Brain Development! Eye Movement Trick That Fixes Trauma! The Secret To EMDR Therapy! - Bessel van Der Kolk

insights.text: The disappearance of religion has led to complexities in living a good life, as we are among the first generations attempting it without religious support.
insights.quote: We are still among the first generations in many parts of the world to be trying to live good lives without the support of religion.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Most Replayed Moment: Alain de Botton - Individualism Is Making Us Miserable!

insights.text: Specific oral bacteria like Fusobacterium nucleatum have been found in breast tissue of women with breast cancer and can make cancer more aggressive.
insights.quote: What they found was that in women who had breast cancer, they had high levels of certain oral bacteria in their breast microbiome. So the oral bacteria is called fusobacterium nucleotum.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Saliva Queen: New Research Linking Bleeding Gums & Breast Cancer! Your Oral Microbiome Can Kill You! If You Can't Get It Up, Brush Your Teeth!

insights.text: Do something that you can deliver and will work your ass off at.
insights.quote: Let me do something that I know that I can deliverand I'll work my ass off at that.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Rochelle Humes: Learning To Be At Peace With Uncertainty

insights.text: True friends are those who support you regardless of your financial situation or professional status.
insights.quote: My true friends... they don't care if I've got five p to my name, they don't care if I've got five millions money, they're my friends.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E12: Sacha Lord's Diary - The King of Nightlife

insights.text: It's critical to focus on and help underrepresented people, so they can be inspired to believe they can achieve great things.
insights.quote: The reason why we now have to promote and push and celebrate and shout about black men and black women and women in general and underrepresented areas of society is that if you are a little kid growing up in Norwich or Manchester or Birmingham or Los Angeles, you have to see those people there to believe that you can get there as well.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Lessons From 50 Of The Worlds Greatest Minds with Jake Humphrey

insights.text: True confidence comes from self-acceptance and self-worth, not external validation.
insights.quote: True confidenceis not, I hope they like me... It's I'm okay and know who I am, even if they don't.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Lucy Hale Opens Up For The First Time About Eating Disorders, Relationships & Addiction

insights.text: Authenticity is key to building a genuine connection and lasting impact on social media.
insights.quote: When you live your life on social media through Instagram and stories every day like you can't hide... I've always just been really honest and really passionate.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E37: Joe Wicks - Changing Millions Of Lives in 15 Seconds!

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley
Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: Sleep is critical for hormonal balance, with even one bad night impacting insulin, cortisol, hunger, and carbohydrate cravings.
insights.quote: One bad night of sleep raises your insulin, raises your cortisol the next day, makes you more hungry, makes you more likely to crave carbohydrates.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Menopause Expert: Belly Fat Grows During Menopause! Your Estrogen Levels Are Controlling You & This Hormone Is Quietly Killing Your Sex Life!

insights.text: Confidence, as culturally defined, is primarily based on evidence, subjective or objective, that individuals possess about themselves.
insights.quote: Confidence as we describe it in culture is really just, um, is based on the evidence you have in yourself.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 86 - How To Turn Your Fear Into Fuel: James Smith

insights.text: Admitting powerlessness and surrendering to a support system is crucial for overcoming addiction.
insights.quote: I don't care what I have to do I will literally do anything to stop feeling like this and NA taught me how to live and how to change and how to heal myself.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Davina McCall: “I was doing cocaine with my mum at 15!”

insights.text: True balance isn't found but actively created by eliminating inessentials and surrounding yourself with people that support your priorities.
insights.quote: You don't find balance, you create it...Get closer to balance by deleting the unessentials.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Man Who Coached Michael Jordan AND Kobe Bryant To WIN!: Tim Grover

insights.text: You don't need to be traditionally 'good at business' to own a successful business; focus on your strengths and hire people better than you at other aspects.
insights.quote: That's when the realization came that you don't need to be good at business to own a business. There's people that are so much better at things than you. They can lay the foundations for it to become a billion dollar brand. And you can focus on what you're actually good at.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - George Heaton (Represent Founder): From My Garden Shed To $100m Business Empire! "Work Life Balance Is Bullshit!", "That Letter Was The End Of Represent!"

insights.text: A single workout can immediately improve mood, focus, and attentional flexibility.
insights.quote: We have new research showing that a single workout can immediately improve your mood, your focus, and your ability to shift and focus attention.
insights.episode_title: E195: The Exercise Neuroscientist: NEW RESEARCH, The Shocking Link Between Exercise And Dementia!

insights.text: Even organic tampons still have pesticides, making them toxic. There is nothing on the market that is a safe feminine hygiene product.
insights.quote: You're using organic, but it's still sprayed with pesticides.You're still putting poison inside of you.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Shocking TRUE Story: “I Lost Both Of My Legs Because Of A Tampon” (Health Warning) - Lauren Wasser

insights.text: The adult entertainment industry can be predatory, particularly towards young women who are offered exploitative contracts that may affect their future.
insights.quote: You're putting contracts in front of 18 year old girls that have the words in perpetuity on them, do you know how dangerous and predatory that is?
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Mia Khalifa's Heart Breaking Reality Of Being Controlled By The Adult Industry

insights.text: Modern society and advertising manipulate our fears to make us buy things we don't need.
insights.quote: Society has manipulated our fight or flight response. It's manipulated our fear response...step one of the advert was create the fear.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E14: John Vincent - Building A Restaurant Empire

insights.text: Caffeine blocks adenosine receptors, temporarily masking sleepiness but causing a build-up of adenosine that leads to a more intense 'caffeine crash' later.
insights.quote: Caffeine is like hitting the mute button on your television remote controller. It just mutes the signal of sleepiness.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 141: What Coffee Is REALLY Doing To Your Sleep!: Matthew Walker

guests: Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: It's crucial to adapt your management style to the current era, or risk being left behind.
insights.quote: So as a coach and as a manager and as a man, I think you need to find a way of how you're moving on with that or you'd be left behind.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - David Moyes Reveals The Truth About Managing The Worlds Greatest Teams

insights.text: Overemphasis on professional work and paid labor has denigrated the importance of raising emotionally, mentally, and physically healthy children.
insights.quote: We have shiftedthis narrative towards working out in the worldand paid labor and accomplishments out in the worldand achievements out in the world,as opposed to what's right in front of you,the accomplishment, which is maybe the greatest accomplishmentof raising emotionally,mentally, physically healthy children that you put out into the world for the next generation.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - THE FEMINISM DEBATE: Is Feminism Betraying Women? The Hidden Risk Of Casual Sex!
insights.guest: Louise Perry, Erica Komisar, Deborah Frances-White

insights.text: When making a decision, make it non-negotiable to see it through; this removes the mental option of quitting and strengthens commitment.
insights.quote: We're making a decision, like Australia, it's non-negotiable. We're not going back on it.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew McConaughey: The Silent Crisis No One Is Talking About! The Harsh Truth About Living Without Faith

insights.text: It's important to re-evaluate your values and ensure your company culture aligns with those values, regardless of having an exit or not.
insights.quote: You can choose right now to re-evaluate like what your values really are. And I think that that was something I didn't do until after the exit.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E15: Gerard Adams - A $50m Elite Exit

insights.text: Reframing failures as learning experiences and skills development is essential for moving forward and achieving greater success.
insights.quote: You get to learn all of these wonderful skills about the nature of the world and people and fundraising. And I'm guessing in many ways that failure had been a blessing because without those two draft misses, you wouldn't have all these amazing skills you have now, right?
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - How I Taught Millions Of Women The Most Important Skill: Girls Who Code Founder: Reshma Saujani

insights.text: A strong personal foundation, a safe space in your mind, is crucial to avoid being defined solely by external achievements and societal expectations.
insights.quote: I wanted to create a safe place in my mind that I could return home to that was not connected to anything external.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Lilly Singh: My Deepest Insecurities Led To My Greatest Achievements

insights.text: In relationships, avoid presenting a false version of yourself and instead be honest about your needs and preferences from the beginning.
insights.quote: So when it comes down to relationships, I'm always trying to, at the start of the relationship, as we all do, you put out this complete false character.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Liam Payne Opens Up About His Darkest Moments, Failed Relationships & Entrepreneurship

insights.text: Acknowledging and addressing past trauma, even years later, is essential for rebuilding a strong sense of self and identity.
insights.quote: Even though it happened 30 years ago, I'm only now just dealing with it because I only found the records... I just, I've spent the last 30 years in this sort of cocoon, not really dealing with a lot of this stuff.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - David Harewood: The Chilling Story Of How A Hollywood Star Lost His Mind

insights.text: Going into a difficult conversation, it is helpful to clarify your purpose, as that can help you remain focused even if the other party becomes aggressive or insulting.
insights.quote: Your mission should keep you on track because you can get pulled from that very quickly.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Secret Service Agent: Never Label Someone A Narcissist! This Habit Makes People Hate Talking To You!

insights.text: It's essential to set a vision for all aspects of your life, similar to running a business, including personal relationships. Treat this like a business scenario - OKRs are important.
insights.quote: I think a smart way to approach your relationship is like you approach your business so you set out a vision for your relationship and you say this is what we want to be and where we want to be in 50 years what does that look like
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Overcoming Depression, Burnout, Anxiety and Insomnia with Dan Murray-Serter

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley
Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: The shame surrounding trauma, rather than the trauma itself, is a significant factor in mental health struggles; releasing this shame can be an antidepressant.
insights.quote: it's not the trauma that destroys you it's the shame about the trauma and giving people ways to release that shame is an antidepressant
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Johann Hari - Everything You Think You Know About Meaning & Happiness Is Wrong

insights.text: Obsession requires attention to detail that others neglect, controlling every manageable aspect to better handle the uncontrollable.
insights.quote: These individuals, they want to control everything they can control so the uncontrollable becomes more manageable to them.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Man Who Coached Michael Jordan AND Kobe Bryant To WIN!: Tim Grover

insights.text: Overcoming distraction and living with personal integrity is the skill of the century.
insights.quote: And I think overcoming distraction, being the kind of person who lives with personal integrity, that is the skill of the century.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E43: Nir Eyal - How to Become Indistractable

insights.text: Traditional medicine has often dismissed women's pain, contributing to delays in diagnosis and treatment for conditions like endometriosis.
insights.quote: I realized this was systemic bias built into the system where women...we call that a whiny woman around here.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Women's Fertility & Lifestyle Debate: Dangers Of Not Having A Period! Fasting Can Backfire For Women
insights.guest: Stacey Sims, Natalie Crawford, Mary Claire Haver, Vonda Wright

insights.text: The only way to truly change actions is by changing the underlying thoughts that drive them.
insights.quote: The only way in which you change actions is by changing thoughts. So we think and then we feel and then we act. That's how we work.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - World Leading Psychologist: How to Succeed in Life & Work - Jamil Qureshi

insights.text: Striving to be the best as an individual, while also prioritizing the team's success, is essential for a healthy competitive environment.
insights.quote: If you want to be as good as you possibly can be, you have to strive to be the best. But can you be okay also with not actually achieving the goal of being the top of everybody, but being as good as you know, you got reached that point of you, you could not have given more.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Strava Founder: How I Motivated 100 Million People To Stay Active: Michael Horvath

insights.text: Connecting with your audience on a deeper level, like through personalized voice notes, can dramatically increase engagement and impact.
insights.quote: The power of a voice note has allowed me to engage, and I now realize that I am on a mission so clear that I'm actually working harder now than when you met me four or five years ago.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E37: Joe Wicks - Changing Millions Of Lives in 15 Seconds!

insights.text: Empathy is crucial for understanding and addressing the root causes of crime and the needs of incarcerated individuals.
insights.quote: There are plenty of people that you can judge for what they've done but in my space you know criminal justice prisons um not everybody in there is a bad person some people have just made mistakes.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Man Wrongfully Convicted Of A Murder He Didn’t Commit: Raphael Rowe

insights.text: Restorative practices such as Non-Sleep Deep Rest (NSDR) can increase baseline dopamine levels, preparing one for action.
insights.quote: We know from a really nice study that NSDR...can increase the baseline levels of dopamine...by about 60% from baseline.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Andrew Huberman: You Must Control Your Dopamine! The Shocking Truth About Cold Showers!

insights.text: Feelings are merely suggestions that can be ignored, providing the freedom to navigate complex situations and ride the waves of experience.
insights.quote: Feelings are merely suggestions, ones you can ignore, but we go through life, no one's ever said that to us before.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Mel Robbins: This One Hack Will Unlock Your Happier Life

guests: Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: Overthinking can hinder creative output; trusting instincts and releasing music without excessive scrutiny can be liberating.
insights.quote: Just release it like it's not that deep just release some music have fun just have fun
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E226: Zara Larsson: How To Stop Caring What Others Think!

insights.text: Small wins can be incredibly meaningful, especially when coming from a place of hardship.
insights.quote: Small wins or little wins in my life were massive... a hug from my mum that felt a little bit like a parental hug rather than a needy or an angry or, that would be a huge, like, I'd done out on that for a month.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Davina McCall: “I was doing cocaine with my mum at 15!”

insights.text: Genuine, specific compliments given individually or in front of a group can significantly boost employee performance and morale.
insights.quote: A genuine compliment with specifics to back it up is the greatest thing in the world and you'll make someone fly and become even stronger the next day.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Barbara Corcoran: Turning $1,000 to $1 Billion!

insights.text: To combat the mental strain, surrounding oneself with joyful experiences and meaningful connections is essential.
insights.quote: If you only try and get happiness from work, for me, it doesn't work. To sit around and hope that your life outside of work can compete with this joy that you get from work. The only way you can do it is to surround yourself with people that you think are fantastic or experiences that you think are fantastic.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Russell Howard: How To Laugh Through Fear, Anxiety & Imposter Syndrome

insights.text: The feeling of 'not belonging' can be lessened by finding and focusing on activities you enjoy.
insights.quote: Focus on finding things you enjoy, like things that you likedoing, activities, and I mean anything, running, playing a sport, reading, like any activitywith your hands.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Trevor Noah: My Depression Was Linked To ADHD! Why I Left The Daily Show!

insights.text: Focus on creating something useful and authentic for your audience to build a strong community and brand.
insights.quote: There's always space for someone to do it in a more authentic better way I think in anything in the world and that's what you've got to do you've got to be comfortable to be yourself not to kind of take ideas from other people back to bring your ideas to the table and really engage with people in a personal way because I think people are inspired by people.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E20: Deliciously Ella - Blogging Saved My Life

insights.text: True optimism in entrepreneurship means refusing to accept 'no' and always seeking a way to achieve goals.
insights.quote: I just think there's always a way there's always a way and your life and my life is testament to this.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - NotOnTheHighStreet.com Founder: Rapid Success Lead To My Darkest Days: Holly Tucker

insights.text: When dealing with addictive behaviours, recognize the two sides of yourself, the one that wants to use the behaviour and the one that wants to be free, and strengthen the latter through consistent effort.
insights.quote: You have to love this thing that you've set up to protect yourself but also you have to talk to it and when you're talking to it you have to understand that there's two sides of yourself and you've got to build up the one that's talking to it.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Richard Osman: The Untold Story Of A TV Legend's Addiction

insights.text: It is better to build trust with an audience than take a large sum of money for something you do not believe in, as that trust will lead to more money in the long run.
insights.quote: No amount of money can make me take a job that I don't believe in...I'd rather build that trust and then take that money because the trust is what will earn you money in the future anyway.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Molly Mae: How She Became Creative Director Of PLT At 22

insights.text: Instead of solely chasing external achievements, focus on treating people well and striving to be a better person, as these are key drivers of personal fulfillment.
insights.quote: In terms of one's own personal sense of fulfillment and self-worth and achievement, I'm more and more convinced that it comes from how you treat people and how they treat you back and how youhow you would try to be a better person
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Stephen Fry: “Lost, alone and I wanted to take my life”

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley

insights.text: High insulin levels, often caused by processed foods and late-night eating, contribute to inflammation and chronic diseases.
insights.quote: The high insulin levels in a healthy person hides that the debris is being made and you don't know it.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Insulin Doctor: This Is The First Sign Of Dementia! The Shocking Link Between Keto & Brain Decline!

insights.text: Small, consistent investments in health yield significant long-term benefits.
insights.quote: Even the smallest investments...will pay off, maybe not even right now, but as you started this with the idea of down the road, like you're realizing now at 32, it's going to matter at 52, 62, 72.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Physiotherapist: You Need To Know This About Creatine! Melt Belly Fat With One Change! The Hidden Cost Of Getting Shredded! - Jeff Cavaliere

insights.text: Diet during pregnancy epigenetically programs the baby's DNA, affecting future health risks like diabetes and psychiatric disorders.
insights.quote: With your diet during pregnancy, you're programming that DNA. And this is going to have an impact on your baby's development and on his future risk of disease.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Pregnancy Diet Expert: The Pregnancy Diet That Rewrites DNA! Why Pregnant Moms Are Being Lied To!

insights.text: Muscle mass is a key determinant of health as we age, even more so than total weight, highlighting the importance of resistance training.
insights.quote: The amount of muscle mass you have...will determine how healthy you are as you age.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Weight Loss Scientist: You've Been LIED To About Calories, Dieting & Losing Weight: Giles Yeo

insights.text: Trauma from both personal experiences and inherited from ancestors can affect mental and physical health.
insights.quote: Your biography becomes your biology.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Longevity Expert: The Truth About Ozempic, The Magic Weight Loss Drug & The Link Between Milk & Cancer!

insights.text: Red stool necessitates medical evaluation due to the potential for colon cancer, which is increasingly affecting younger populations.
insights.quote: If you see blood in your stool, that's not normal. And to me, that's grounds to talk to a doctor...colon cancer is the numbertwo cause of cancer death in our countries.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 195: 10 Things Your STOOL Says About Your Health: The No.1 Poo & Gut Scientist

insights.text: Consistency is key for fitness results, whether it's a small or large time investment.
insights.quote: It's the consistency that matters. It doesn't matter if it's two hours a week or if it's 18 hours a week. If you're consistent, you can get amazing benefits.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Muscle Building Expert: “Creatine Loading Is a Waste Of Time!”, They’re Lying To You About Workout Hours! Dr Michael Israetel

insights.text: Consuming a diverse range of plants is crucial for fueling a healthy gut microbiome, aiming for at least 30 different plants per week.
insights.quote: The way to lift the microbes up is by eating a variety of plants.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Poo Scientist: "If Your Poo Looks Like This Go To A Doctor!", "Your Gut Health Causes Belly Fat,  Anxiety!" & "Alcohol Is Destroying Your Gut Microbiome!"

insights.text: Peak muscle mass is typically reached around age 30, making the mid-30s to mid-40s a crucial period to invest in health and prevent decline.
insights.quote: Some scientists, muscle scientists believe that at 30, we've got our peak muscle mass.And unless we really invest in growing more muscle the rest of our lives...you peaked at 30 and you're using it the rest of your life.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Healthy Ageing Doctor: Doing This For 30s Will Burn More Fat Than A Long Run! Sitting Is The Silent Killer! - Vonda Wright

insights.text: Fermented foods are a natural source of probiotics, providing a wider range of microbes than supplements.
insights.quote: The difference between this and your probiotic capsule is that you'll probably get 30 different microbes here. Whereas you take your capsule, you might get two or three.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Food Doctor: "Extra Protein Is Making You Fatter!" The 6 Food Lies Everyone Still Believes! (Brand NEW Food Science) - Tim Spector

insights.text: Women doing fasted training may burn lean mass because their hypothalamus is more sensitive to nutrient density compared to men.
insights.quote: All you've effectively done is burn through your lean mass...It would be better for you as a woman to have maybe 15 grams of protein...before you go do cardio and strength.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Exercise & Nutrition Scientist: The Truth About Exercising On Your Period! Women Were Right About Menopause! These 4 Supplements Give Women Optimal Health!

insights.text: Grip strength is an important, often overlooked marker for overall health and can be predictive of Alzheimer's and dementia risk.
insights.quote: You can't not pay attention to grip strength. And in fact, we can actually predict Alzheimer's and dementia risk via grip strength testing.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Muscle Growth Doctor: "The Anti-Ageing Cure No One Is Talking About!", "Exercising At Night Is A Terrible Idea!", "Your Grip Strength Predicts Chronic Diseases!" - Andy Galpin

insights.text: The gut microbiome plays a vital role in the efficacy of immunotherapy treatments, specifically the presence of *Akkermansia muciniphila*.
insights.quote: The research that was presented showed that there was no differences between the groups of responders...except for one thing that one thing was one bacteria the responders had one bacteria called acromansiamucinophila.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Chronic Disease Expert: We Can Now Reverse Stage 4 Cancer! This Common Food Is Feeding Your Cancer Cells!

insights.text: Stress can lead to abdominal fat that's difficult to lose through diet and exercise alone because it is tied to cortisol levels.
insights.quote: As long as you're still leaking out extra cortisol, nothing's going to change. So, and like I said, even exercising more or eating better, less or differently, whatever it is, wouldn't shift that fat you had to get to the root cause
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 156: Your Stress Is Making you Fat & Sick... Here's How To Fix It!
